baseline_update_protocol: If output changed, produce a human-readable diff (field, old, new, scenario id).
Classify the change: intended, unintended, or unclear.
Update expected outputs only when the change is intended and documented.
Store baseline metadata: corpus version, engine/schema version, seed/fixture id, generation command.

agent_split.codex: Implementation driver — service, store, and Cloud Functions logic.

anti_goals: No ECS/data-oriented refactor unless independently justified.
No broad cleanup mixed into verification sprints.

owner: Kevin Bigham — high school teacher, head swim/dive coach at Blue Springs HS, founder of Blue Springs Power Cats swim team
working_style: Kevin directs AI agents but doesn't write code. Terse updates at milestones, not play-by-play. Once direction is clear, assume-label-proceed. Match his energy. No emoji in UIs — use lucide-react icons. Tests expected on all code.
multi_agent_system: 4-agent workflow: ChatGPT (architect), Codex (builder), Claude Code (reviewer/ops), Kevin (director). Handoffs via JSON payloads and .codex/ docs.

stack.framework: React Native 0.81.5 + Expo SDK 54 + Expo Router 6
stack.backend: Firebase JS SDK 12 (Firestore, Auth, Storage, Cloud Functions v2)
stack.ai: Google Cloud Vertex AI — Gemini 2.0 Flash via Cloud Functions
stack.testing: Jest 29 + jest-expo + @testing-library/react-native (858 client tests across 87 suites; 65 functions tests across 12 suites)
stack.ci_cd: GitHub Actions — typecheck, lint, test, EAS build, functions deploy

theme.name: Arcade Prime Time
theme.description: Dark broadcast studio aesthetic with retro arcade accents
theme.rule: No emoji in UIs — use lucide-react-native icons only

key_collections.coaches: Auth-linked coach profiles with roles and notification prefs
key_collections.swimmers: Roster with subcollections: notes, times, goals, medical
key_collections.audio_sessions: Recorded coaching audio with AI transcription drafts
key_collections.video_sessions: Uploaded video with AI technique analysis drafts
key_collections.practice_plans: Practice plans with sets/items, also serves as workout library
key_collections.meets: Competitions with subcollections: entries, relays, live_events, splits
key_collections.notification_rules: Custom alert triggers for attendance streaks, etc.
key_collections.parent_invites: Shareable invite codes for parent app linking

ai_features.audio_transcription: Record coaching audio → Cloud Function transcribes via Gemini → extracts per-swimmer observations → coach reviews/approves → posts to swimmer notes
ai_features.video_analysis: Upload poolside video → Cloud Function sends to Gemini → generates technique observations with diagnosis + drill recommendations → coach reviews → posts to swimmer notes
ai_features.practice_generation: Coach selects group + focus + yardage → Cloud Function generates structured plan via Gemini with age-appropriate sets and intervals
ai_features.pipeline: All AI results go through a draft review system (approve/reject) before posting to swimmer profiles

offline_support.upload_queue: AsyncStorage-backed queue (src/utils/offlineQueue.ts) with 3 retries for audio/video uploads
offline_support.indicator: OfflineIndicator component shows amber banner when disconnected
offline_support.queue_processor: Mounted in _layout.tsx — processes queue on NetInfo connectivity restore

critical_patterns.time_format: All times stored as hundredths of seconds (6523 = 1:05.23)
critical_patterns.firebase_timestamp: Typed as Date but runtime is Firestore Timestamp — use toDate() helper
critical_patterns.pr_detection: Auto-detected in times.ts addTime() — old PRs un-flagged when new PR recorded
critical_patterns.batch_limit: All bulk writes chunk at 400 (Firestore limit is 500)
critical_patterns.subscription_cleanup: All services return unsubscribe functions; stores manage single subscription
critical_patterns.relay_optimization: Free relay order: 2nd, slowest, 3rd, fastest (anchor). Medley: greedy by best stroke times

completed_phases.description: Full app foundation — 49 screens, 28 services, Arcade Prime Time theme
Developer tooling — ESLint, Prettier, Husky, zero any types
Testing infrastructure — Jest, RNTL, Firebase mocks
Core test suite — 437 tests across 37 suites
Error handling — ErrorBoundary, ScreenErrorBoundary, logger, withRetry
Race analytics, doc export, notification rules
App Store prep — privacy policy, terms of service
Wire analytics to real Firestore data, fix calendar collection
Firestore rules hardening, Sentry, babel console strip, v1.2.0
DOCX export for practice plans and reports

future_directions.parent_portal: parent-portal/ directory exists but is not built out yet. Parents can redeem invite codes via Cloud Function. Need: parent-facing app with swimmer view, meet schedule, push notifications
future_directions.lcm_scm_standards: Only SCY time standards populated in src/data/timeStandards.ts — need LCM and SCM data
future_directions.aggregations_pipeline: Cloud Functions should populate the aggregations collection for dashboard stats (currently computed client-side)
future_directions.e2e_tests: No Detox/Maestro tests — could add for critical flows (check-in, recording, meet management)
future_directions.deep_linking: URL scheme bspc-coach:// registered but no handlers configured
future_directions.eas_submit: Apple/Google store credentials need filling in eas.json after enrollment
future_directions.workout_sharing: workoutLibrary service has rating/tagging but no cross-coach sharing
future_directions.schedule_auto_pull: Calendar was manually seeded from bspowercats.com — could automate
future_directions.push_topics: Basic FCM setup — could add group-based and event-based notification topics
future_directions.attendance_trends: Notification rules exist for streaks but trend detection could be richer
future_directions.meet_live_mode: Timer and live event tracking exist — could add spectator view and live results publishing

documentation.full_overview: .codex/FULL_APP_OVERVIEW.md — complete file map, service catalog, collection map
documentation.architecture: Memory file: project_architecture_decision.md — Firebase stack decision rationale
